data.title: Tesla rallies again and takes Elon Musk to unchartered heights for net worth
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) rose to a new multi-year high of $415.00 midday action on Wednesday. The electric vehicle stock has more than doubled over the last four months to extend its market cap to over $1.3 trillion.

Of course, a higher share price for Tesla (TSLA) is also good for Elon Musk, who is now considered to be the first person to ever have a net worth of over $400 billion. On top of his Tesla (TSLA) stake, Musk is also an owner of SpaceX (SPACE), X, Neuralink, The Boring Company, and xAI.

The biggest buzz this week around Tesla (TSLA) is data showing China demand may be stronger than anticipated and a Deutsche Bank report that claims that EV makes has plans to release a new model in the first half of 2025. The firm thinks the new Tesla (TSLA) model will cost less than $30,000 with subsidies, unless incoming President Donald Trump cancels the IRA tax credit.

Tesla (TSLA) is the 8th largest company traded in the U.S. by market cap. With another 15% rally, the company will be more valuable than Meta Platforms (META) and in 7th place on the market cap list.

data.title: Honeywell upgraded at HSBC on possibility of breakup
data.description:
Honeywell International (NASDAQ:HON) on Wednesday was upgraded to Buy from a previous investment rating of Hold by analysts at financial-services firm HSBC. They said the aerospace and defense company has the potential to create more value for investors by splitting its aerospace and automation businesses into separate companies.

“We like Honeywell (HON) for its diversified portfolio, which should help it perform well in the current economic environment, with decent organic growth and margin expansion,” Wesley Brooks, analyst at HSBC, said in a December 11 report.

HSBC pointed to Honeywell’s (HON) return on invested capital gains of 23% for the period from 2014 to 2023, and free cash flow conversion of about 97%. The company returned 104% of its free cash flow in the form of dividends, 44%, and share buybacks, 60%, according to HSBC’s analysis.

Honeywell’s (HON) spinoff of its advanced materials business and the sale of its personal protective equipment unit “should help in the re-rating of the stock,” HSBC said.

HSBC raised its price target on Honeywell (HON) to $290 a share from $235 a share previously, based on an estimated multiple of 26.6 times 12-month forward earnings per share.

data.title: Nucor raises dividend by 1.9% to $0.55
data.description:
• Nucor (NYSE:NUE) declares $0.55/share quarterly dividend, 1.9% increase from prior dividend of $0.54.
  • Forward yield 1.58%
  • Payable Feb. 11; for shareholders of record Dec. 31; ex-div Dec. 31.
  • See NUE Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Missouri Senator Hawley aims to stop arbitrary and discriminatory airline fees
data.description:
A new bill introduced by Senator Josh Hawley (R-MO) would restrict discriminatory airline fares or fees based on protected characteristics and ban carriers from paying employees incentives for charging passengers extra fees for baggage, seat selection, or other optional amenities as a condition to boarding a flight.

Hawley’s “End Airline Extortion” bill, introduced into Congress on Tuesday, seeks to end the “disastrous” policies enacted by U.S. carriers that has made travel a “horrible and terrible experience” for flyers, the Missouri senator said. Hawley called out executives from Frontier Group Holdings (NASDAQ:ULCC), and Spirit Airlines (OTC:SAVEQ) specifically for their egregious luggage policies during the Senate's questioning of airline executives last week.

“You made clear that money is your bottom line—I get that. But why is it that you’re charging some people, triple, quadruple the amount [for luggage]?” the senator asked, accusing carriers of paying gate agents a total of $26M in incentives between 2022 and 2023 to “harass customers whose bags were too big.” Frontier (ULCC) gate agents can earn as much as $10 for each bag a passenger is forced to check at the gate.

During a contentious and heated questioning of Spirit (OTC:SAVEQ) chief commercial officer Matthew Klein as to why some passengers are charged more for luggage on the same flight, Klein responded, “we want to make sure we get the best price to the best people.” When grilled by Hawley, “who are the best people?” Klein rephrased his answer but acknowledged that the fees were arbitrary.

The testimony of airline executives was part of a bipartisan Senate Permanent Subcommittee Investigation to address airlines’ junk fees to boost airline revenues.

In 2023, industry data shows that domestic carriers made $90B on ancillary fees. These include extra charges for baggage, seat selection and onboard meals, making these fees a critical revenue stream for ultra-low-cost carriers like Spirit (OTC:SAVEQ) and Frontier (ULCC). Additionally, the subcommittee found that by labeling portions of their charges as nontaxable fees, Spirit (OTC:SAVEQ), Frontier (ULCC) and United (UAL) appear to have avoided paying a 7.5% transportation excise tax on the fees.

Despite the ramifications on airlines’ bottom-line should the legislation pass, shares of Spirit (OTC:SAVEQ), Frontier (ULCC), and Allegiant Travel (ALGT) are all gaining altitude with the help of recent CPI data showing a 4.7% increase in airfares in November from last year.

data.title: Google requests FTC to break up Microsoft-OpenAI's cloud deal - report
data.description:
Update: The story was updated with Microsoft's response of declining to comment on the report.

Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) unit Google has requested the U.S. Federal Trade Commission to break up Microsoft's (NASDAQ:MSFT) exclusive deal to host OpenAI's technology on its cloud servers, The Information reported.

The discussions occurred after the U.S. regulator asked Google about Microsoft's business practices as part of a broader probe, the report added, citing a person involved in the talks.

"We have no comment," FTC said in an email to Seeking Alpha.

Microsoft declined to comment on the matter.

Microsoft's competitors in the cloud server space, such as Google, also want to host OpenAI's large language models, or LLMs, so that their cloud customers do not need to access Microsoft's servers to get access to the ChatGPT-maker's technology, the report noted.

Companies that buy OpenAI's technology via Microsoft may have to see additional charges if they do not already use Microsoft's servers to carry out their operations, the report added.

Google and other rivals have highlighted that these new costs will impact customers, the report noted.

Google, OpenAI and the FTC did not respond to an email from Seeking Alpha till the time of publication of this report.

data.title: Nasdaq up 1%, S&P climbs as November inflation data strengthen Fed rate-cut bets
data.description:
U.S. stocks rose Wednesday, after an in-line consumer inflation report cemented expectations of another quarter-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week.

After the November inflation report was released, odds of the Fed delivering its third rate cut in 2024 climbed to 96% from 89% a day earlier, according to the CME FedWatch tool. That helped warm up the S&P 500 (SP500) and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) toward each halting a two-day slide.

The benchmark (SP500) +0.9% at 6,086.64 points, and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) +1.6% at 19,995.19 points. The Dow (DJI) gauge of blue-chip stocks +0.1% at 44,304.74 points.

Six of the 11 sectors on the S&P 500 (SP500) rose, topped by the Communication Services +2.3%, Consumer Discretionary and Information Tech groups. All the Magnificent 7 tech behemoths climbed, with Tesla (TSLA) +3% touching its highest price since 2021.

The consumer price index (CPI) for November from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics rose 0.3% M/M, the highest reading since April and matching the consensus estimate. Headline CPI climbed 2.7% Y/Y. Core CPI at +0.3% M/M was as expected, and it increased 3.3% on a Y/Y basis.

"The increase in the inflation rate (2.7% vs 2.6%) won’t be enough to spoil Christmas – the Fed is going to cut rates another 25 bps next week and that should enable markets to rally into year-end," Chris Zaccarelli, chief investment officer at Northlight Asset Management, said. The "series is a little noisy from month-to-month," he said, but "we believe the Fed is likely to look through these fluctuations and continue on their easing path."

Treasury yields initially fell after the CPI report but later turned mixed. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved up 2 basis points to 4.25%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 1 basis point at 4.15%.

The Federal Open Market Committee at its December 17-18 meeting is widely expected to reduce the fed funds rate to 4.25%-4.5%. November's CPI report indicates there is still work to do for policymakers in terms of getting inflation to move further toward their 2% target.

"Today's inflation data probably do not represent a sea change in the outlook for the Federal Reserve. Over the course of 2024, inflation has continued to slow, albeit more gradually than many had hoped it would at the start of the year," Wells Fargo's Sarah House said.

"We believe the FOMC will continue to reduce the federal funds rate next year in an effort to move monetary policy to a less restrictive position. We look for 100 bps of rate cuts from the FOMC over the next 12 months, with 25 bps rate cuts at next week's FOMC meeting and the March, June and September meetings next year," House added.

The U.S. bull run had stalled over the past two days ahead of the November CPI data. Still, Wall Street is on track to end another year with hefty gains.

Wednesday's spotlight was also grabbed by a few key earnings reports. Macy's (M) -5.9% after the department store chain delivered a quarterly decline in comparable sales and cut its annual adjusted profit per share guidance.

Heavily shorted GameStop (GME) +9%, after the video game retailer delivered a surprise quarterly profit.

Adobe (ADBE) stock will be in focus ahead of the creative software maker's results after the closing bell.

data.title: Apollo CEO Rowan sees 'unbelievable' opportunities ahead in private markets
data.description:
Apollo Global Management (NYSE:APO) CEO Marc Rowan sees "an unbelievable set of opportunities" ahead for private market investing with the upcoming change in the White House administration.

Already, the environment has changed. "Everyone is more relaxed," he said during an interview at an industry conference on Wednesday.

And even though though the political environment was hostile to private markets during the Biden administration, "we prospered mightily over the past four years," he said.

"I'm very optimistic we will see change there, which does not mean private equity, hedge funds, venture capital into people's retirement funds," Rowan said. "We're down to 4,000 public companies, and the vast majority of our economy is private. Long-term investors will find a way to participate in the economy."

He wouldn't be surprised to see a $10B-$20B deal in private assets, he added.

Banks will also benefit, he added. "Whether there's reduced regulation or just a change in tone, it is clearly going to be a better environment for the banking system."

The biggest opportunity is in levering private assets. "The notion of actually levering private assets — and I don't mean on a risk basis, I mean on an investment-grade basis with appropriate haircuts — I think could be the single biggest opportunity that exists for firms like ours," he said.

Rowan is seeing that already as banks pull back from lending to clients against their private portfolios. Apollo (NYSE:APO) is entering partnerships with banks "where the banks are now maintaining the relationships with their client. They're making loans, but those loans are with our money," Rowan said.

"The best risk-reward I see in any asset class is hybrid (investments)," he said.

Litigation relief is needed to add private assets to 401(k)s, he said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Anaptys scraps development of ANB032, stock plunges 33%
data.description:
Anaptys Bio (NASDAQ:ANAB) stock plunged 33% Wednesday after the company said it was scrapping further development of its drug candidate ANB032 due to disappointing clinical results.

The biotech company said a Phase 2b study of ANB032 failed to meet its primary or secondary endpoints in the treatment of atopic dermatitis. As a result, it was discontinuing both the study and further development of the drug, according to a statement.

Anaptys added that it expects to report topline Phase 2b data for its drug rosnilimab in the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis in February.

The company sees having cash of approximately $415M at the end of the year, with a cash runway through 2027, excluding any potential milestone or royalty payments from GSK (GSK).

data.title: Football win-win: Nike extends its deal with the NFL out to 2038
data.description:
Nike (NYSE:NKE) inked an extension to its landmark 10-year partnership with the National Football League on Wednesday. The development puts an end to speculation that the NFL will sign a deal with another sports apparel company.

The Beaverton, Oregon-based company said it is prepared to build on 12 years of successful collaboration as the exclusive provider of uniforms and sideline, practice and base layer apparel for all 32 NFL teams.

"With a relentless pursuit of innovation and a deep understanding of the unique needs of NFL players, Nike will deliver cutting-edge, high-performance products that adapt to the evolving demands of the game's elite athletes. As a result, the partnership will not only shape the future of the sport but also drive a new era of excellence in football -- fostering a global, inclusive, and safer environment for football players of all levels to thrive."

Key initiatives of the Nike (NKE)-NFL partnership include a commitment to grow the game's global reach, increasing participation, developing new talent, and expanding the football fan base. Nike (NKE) said it will continue to empower the next generation of athletes and grow the sport by supporting grassroots, high school and collegiate level youth development programs across both flag and tackle football.

NFL Commissioner Roger Goodell noted that Nike (NKE) has been an invaluable partner since 2012. "In addition to their products and services for our clubs, players, or fans, Nike is a strategic partner who will help us grow football internationally, support youth football and make advances in player safety," he highlighted.

Some of the most notable NFL players endorsed by Nike (NKE) are Patrick Mahomes, Josh Allen, Travis Kelce, and Russell Wilson.

Shares of Nike (NKE) were up 2.45% in late morning trading.

data.title: Google launches AI model Gemini 2, heating up rivalry with OpenAI
data.description:
Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) Google unveiled Gemini 2.0 Flash experimental model on Wednesday, heating up tech competition with OpenAI with its latest AI model.

The U.S. tech giant is also launching a new feature called Deep Research, which uses advanced reasoning and long context capabilities to act as a research assistant, exploring complex topics and compiling reports on users' behalf. It will be available to Gemini Advanced subscribers from today.

Google said it is bringing Gemini 2.0 to AI Overviews to tackle more complex topics and multi-step questions, including advanced math equations, multimodal queries and coding. The company added that it began limited testing this week and will roll it out more broadly early next year. Google also plans to bring AI Overviews to more countries and languages over the next year.

Google noted that Gemini 2.0 is built on custom hardware like Trillium, its sixth-generation Tensor Processing Units, or TPUs. TPUs powered 100% of Gemini 2.0 training and inference.

Starting today, the Trillium chip will be generally available to customers, according to the company.

Gemini 2.0 Flash builds on the success of 1.5 Flash, the company's most popular model for developers.

Google said 2.0 Flash outperforms 1.5 Pro on key benchmarks, at twice the speed. Besides supporting multimodal inputs like images, video and audio, 2.0 Flash now supports multimodal output like natively generated images mixed with text and steerable text-to-speech, or TTS, multilingual audio.

The new version can also natively call tools like Google Search, code execution, and third-party user-defined functions.

Google has been facing competition from OpenAI in the AI race.

In October, Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI said it was bringing a new set of search features to ChatGPT, while last month it was reported that the startup considered launching its own web browser to challenge Google Chrome. OpenAI is also pushing its monetization efforts with a new $200 per month plan. Reportedly, the company was in talks to remove an 'AGI' provision with Microsoft to unlock potential investments. Google has also been upping its AI game amid challenge from AI rivals. In October, the search giant announced that it was bringing the Gemini AI chatbot to its Maps application.

Over the past month, Google has shared early, experimental versions of Gemini 2.0 with developers and received "great feedback."

Gemini 2.0 Flash is now available as an experimental model to developers via the Gemini Application Programming Interface, or API, in Google AI Studio and Vertex AI with multimodal input and text output available to all developers, and text-to-speech and native image generation available to early-access partners.

The company added that general availability will happen in January, along with more model sizes.

Google is also releasing a new Multimodal Live API which has real-time audio, video-streaming input and the ability to use multiple, combined tools, to help developers build interactive applications.

In addition, Google said that from today, Gemini users globally can access a chat optimized version of 2.0 Flash experimental on desktop and mobile web.

It will be available in the Gemini mobile app soon, as per the company.

Early next year, the company will expand Gemini 2.0 to more Google products.

Project Astra: Google's research prototype which explores future capabilities of a universal AI assistant.

Google introduced Project Astra at its I/O event. The improvements in the latest version built with Gemini 2.0 include — Project Astra can converse in multiple languages and in mixed languages, with a better understanding of accents and uncommon words.

With Gemini 2.0, Project Astra can use Google Search, Lens and Maps. It now has better memory, with up to 10 minutes of in-session memory, it can remember more conversations a user had with it in the past. It also has improved latency, as the agent can understand language at about the latency of human conversation.

Project Mariner is an early research prototype built with Gemini 2.0 which explores the future of human-agent interaction, starting with the browser.

It can understand and reason across information on the browser screen, including pixels items such as text, code, images and forms, and then uses this information via an experimental Chrome extension to complete tasks for the user.

Google noted that to build this safely and responsibly, its is carrying out active research on new types of risks and mitigations.

Project Mariner can only type, scroll or click in the active tab on the browser and it asks users for final confirmation before taking certain actions, such as buying something.

Jules: It is an experimental AI-powered code agent for developers that integrates directly into a GitHub workflow.
